steps.title: To prevent cross contamination, nucleic acid...
steps.instruction: To prevent cross contamination, nucleic acid extraction and amplification (PCR) should be performed in separate rooms. Processing can be done prior to freezing samples to save freezer space. Each location/site would account for 4 filter paper water samples for each sampling site. Wipe the surfaces with 70% ethanol to remove contaminants.

steps.title: Use tubing to connect a 3...
steps.instruction: Use tubing to connect a 3 L Medi-Vac Canister with vacuum pump through the vacuum outlet on the lid. (If possible, the canister should be set up inside a biosafety cabinet).

steps.title: Connect tubing with the 3 L...
steps.instruction: Connect tubing with the 3 L Medi-Vac Canister through the port for air-in (indicated as patient) on the lid. Close unused inlets. Turn on the pump to test the vacuum suction by feeling the airflow.

steps.title: When water sample is very dirty,...
steps.instruction: When water sample is very dirty, filter the water sample with a sterile 8-ply gauze on a funnel using gravity to remove floating plants, mud, and microalgae, it could be done multiple times. Assemble Magnetic Filter funnel, tubing, GL45 Screw Cap with 2-hose connector, 1 L dry glass bottle (autoclaved or bleach rinsed) and the Medi-Vac Canister as Appendix 1. See Appendix 1 for the water filtration system setting. Place the Magnetic filter Funnel at a position higher than the 1 L clean glass bottle, any way will do if the funnel is secure.

steps.title: If the water samples have high...
steps.instruction: If the water samples have high turbidity, settle water at 4 °C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Wipe the filter holder of the...
steps.instruction: Wipe the filter holder of the magnetic funnel with 70% ethanol and let ethanol air dry.

steps.title: Place a 30 µm filter membrane...
steps.instruction: Place a 30 µm filter membrane disc on the filter holder.

steps.title: Attach the top funnel cup to the filter holder
steps.instruction: Attach the top funnel cup to the filter holder.

steps.title: Pour the settled water sample to...
steps.instruction: Pour the settled water sample to the magnetic funnel cup, avoid disturbing the precipitation as much as possible.

steps.title: Turn on the pump (<15 psi)...
steps.instruction: Turn on the pump (<15 psi) to allow the water sample to pass through the filter and be collected in the bottle (if the pump flow rate cannot be controlled, put a tube clip on the air outlet tube to control the flow rate avoiding the water splash in the bottle). Turn off the vacuum pump after the water sample runs out (If clogging happens, replace the membrane disc filter with a new one and collect all the filtrates in the same bottle).

steps.title: Discard the 30 µm filter membrane disc
steps.instruction: Discard the 30 µm filter membrane disc.

steps.title: Pour the filtrate to a sterile...
steps.instruction: Pour the filtrate to a sterile sampling bag with flat-wire closure or a clean bottle and connect the bottle back to the filtration system.

steps.title: Place a new 10 µm filter...
steps.instruction: Place a new 10 µm filter membrane on the filter holder and filter the filtrate as step 9-10. Avoid using multiple 10 µm filter membranes to finish the filtration (the plastic bag can be reused to contain the same sample in the second round of the filtration).

steps.title: Store the 10 µm filter membrane...
steps.instruction: Store the 10 µm filter membrane in a sterile 60 mm Petri dish and keep the dish On ice . Label the Petri dish with sample ID, filtration date, and membrane size.

steps.title: Place a 5 µm filter membrane...
steps.instruction: Place a 5 µm filter membrane on the membrane holder then repeat step 9-10. Avoid using multiple 5 µm filter membranes to finish the filtration.

steps.title: After overnight incubation with PEG-8000, water...
steps.instruction: After overnight incubation with PEG-8000, water samples are ready for viral particle capturing filtration.

steps.title: Assemble the filtration system following the...
steps.instruction: Assemble the filtration system following the steps described in steps 4-6.

steps.title: Place a new 5 µm filter...
steps.instruction: Place a new 5 µm filter membrane on the filter holder and filter the filtrate as steps 9-10. Avoid using multiple 5 µm filter membranes to finish the filtration.

steps.title: Store the 5 µm filter membrane...
steps.instruction: Store the 5 µm filter membrane in a sterile 60 mm Petri dish and keep the dish On ice . Label the Petri dish with sample ID, filtration date, and membrane size.

steps.title: Pour the filtrate to a sterile...
steps.instruction: Pour the filtrate to a sterile sampling bag with flat-wire closure or a clean bottle and connect the bottle back to the filtration system.

steps.title: Place a new 0
steps.instruction: Place a new 0.45 µm filter membrane on the membrane holder and filter the filtrate as steps 9-10. Avoid using multiple 0.45 µm filter membranes to finish the filtration.

steps.title: Store the 0
steps.instruction: Store the 0.45 µm filter membrane in a sterile 60 mm Petri dish and keep the dish on ice. Label the Petri dish with sample ID, filtration date, and membrane size.

steps.title: Pre-cool the Bullet Blender by adding...
steps.instruction: Pre-cool the Bullet Blender by adding dry On ice into the cooling compartment and running the cooling program.

steps.title: Clean the work surfaces with RNaseZap,...
steps.instruction: Clean the work surfaces with RNaseZap, then wipe the surfaces with 70% molecular biology grade ethanol to remove additional contaminants.

steps.title: Transfer 0
steps.instruction: Transfer 0.1 mm zirconium oxide beads (2 spoons, Appendix 4) and four 3.5 mm UFO beads to Thermo Scientific Screw Cap 1.5 mL Micro Tubes. Each water sample needs two bead tubes. Can be prepared in advance as described in Before Start.

steps.title: Add 650 µL of ATL-DX buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 650 µL of ATL-DX buffer and 135 µL VXL buffer to the Thermo Scientific Screw Cap 1.5 mL Micro Tubes containing 0.1 mm and 3.5 mm UFO beating beads. For the preparation of the ATL-DX buffer, see step "Buffer ATL may form precipitates upon storage. If necessary, warm to 56 °C until the precipitates have fully dissolved. Prepare buffer ATL-DX: add 100 µL Reagent DX to 15 mL Buffer ATL. If smaller amounts are needed, transfer 1.5 mL of Buffer ATL into a sterile 2 mL vial and add 10 µL Reagent DX." in before start section.

steps.title: Each water sample has 4 filter...
steps.instruction: Each water sample has 4 filter membranes from different filtrations (before PEG-8000 treatment: membrane of pore size 10 and 5, one of each; after PEG-8000 treatment: membrane of pore size 5 and 0.45, one of each)

steps.title: Use 70% ethanol to wipe forceps...
steps.instruction: Use 70% ethanol to wipe forceps and surgical scissors (or use new razor blade).

steps.title: Trim off the outer circle of...
steps.instruction: Trim off the outer circle of the membrane that had no water sample flowing through, discard the outer circle (see Figure 1).

steps.title: Cut the membranes into 2 halves
steps.instruction: Cut the membranes into 2 halves.

steps.title: Place 4 halves of the filter...
steps.instruction: Place 4 halves of the filter membranes from different filtrations of the same water sample in a new Petri dish and store the unused half membranes in the original Petri dish at -20 °C for future use (see Figure 1).

steps.title: Use the forceps to stack the...
steps.instruction: Use the forceps to stack the 2 half membranes, then fold the stacked halves into a smaller sector and cut it (smaller than 1 mm x 3 mm , see example in Figure 1) into the a tube prepared in step 40 (Suggest collecting the 2 half membranes before PEG-8000 in tube A and the 2 half membranes from after PEG-8000 treatment in tube B).

steps.title: Add 20 µL Proteinase K from...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL Proteinase K from IndiMag kit and incubate the tube at 56 °C in the heat block shaker set up at 1400 rpm, , 20 minutes (if heat block shaker is not available, vortex the tube every 00:05:00 ).

steps.title: Confirm 96 deep-well magnetic head and...
steps.instruction: Confirm 96 deep-well magnetic head and 96 well deep-well heat block are being used.

steps.title: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash has been...
steps.instruction: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash has been downloaded and loaded onto the KingFisher Flex instrument.

steps.title: Set up the Wash, Elution, and...
steps.instruction: Set up the Wash, Elution, and Tip Comb Plates outside the instrument according to the following table. DO NOT use the elution buffer provided by the kit for TNA elution. The ingredients in the elution buffer inhibit the downstream DNA sequencing efficiency. %s %s Plate ID Plate position Plate type Reagent Volume per well Tip comb 7 Place a 96 Deep-well Tip comb in a deep-well plate Elution 6 Deep-Well Nuclease-free water 75 µL Wash 4 5 Deep-Well 100 % ethanol 750 µL Wash 3 4 Deep-Well 80% ethanol 750 µL Wash 2 3 Deep-Well Buffer AW2 700 µL Wash 1 2 Deep-Well Buffer AW1 700 µL Sample 1 Sample Lysate Lysate and lysis buffer 985 µL

steps.title: Transfer 425 µL supernatant from step...
steps.instruction: Transfer 425 µL supernatant from step 52 without any particle carryover to the wells of the Deep-well plate. This plate becomes the Sample Plate.

steps.title: Add 540 µL Buffer ACB, and...
steps.instruction: Add 540 µL Buffer ACB, and 20 µL MagAttract Suspension G to each sample in the sample plate. For multiple samples, make a master mix with 10% overage. Invert slowly to mix the master mix, avoid foaming (can be mixed on Hula mixer for 00:02:00 ). Add 560 µL mixture to each sample.

steps.title: Select the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash on the instrument
steps.instruction: Select the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Flex_4wash on the instrument.

steps.title: Start the run, then load the...
steps.instruction: Start the run, then load the prepared plates into the positions when prompted by the instrument.

steps.title: After the running protocol is completed...
steps.instruction: After the running protocol is completed (~ 00:35:00 ), immediately remove the elution plate from the instrument and cover the plate or transfer the eluate to the final tube or plate of choice for final storage. The elutes from the 2 bead tubes of the same water sample can be pooled in one final tube.

steps.title: In a 0
steps.instruction: In a 0.6 mL microcentrifuge tube, use 3 µL total nucleic acid for DNA and RNA concentration measurement using Qubit 4 Fluorometer following manufacturer instructions(Kits needed: Qubit 1X dsDNA HS Assay Kit and Qubit RNA HS Assay Kit) (see Appendix 5 and Appendix 6).

steps.title: Proceed with sample testing following the...
steps.instruction: Proceed with sample testing following the REDI-NET SOP W-4 Water Testing or store at -20 °C for less than 2 weeks (for long-term storage the sample needs to be stored at -80 °C following the REDI-NET SOP W-3 Water Storage).

steps.title: Confirm 12-tip magnetic head and 12...
steps.instruction: Confirm 12-tip magnetic head and 12 deep-well heat blocks are being used.

steps.title: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash has been...
steps.instruction: Ensure the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash has been downloaded and loaded onto the KingFisher Duo Prime instrument.

steps.title: Set up the Sample Plate according to the table below
steps.instruction: Set up the Sample Plate according to the table below: %s %s Row ID Plate Row Reagent Volume per well Sample row A Lysate and lysis buffer 985 µL Wash 1 B Buffer AW1 700 µL Wash 2 C Buffer AW2 700 µL Wash 3 D 80 % ethanol 750 µL Wash 4 E 100 % ethanol 750 µL Tip Comb Wash 2 F 12-Tip comb G Empty H

steps.title: Set up the Elution Strip according to the table below
steps.instruction: Set up the Elution Strip according to the table below: NOTE: DO NOT use the elution buffer provided by the kit for TNA elution. The ingredients in the elution buffer inhibit the downstream DNA sequencing efficiency. %s %s Row ID Plate Row Reagent Volume per well Elution A Nuclease-free water 75 µL

steps.title: Centrifuge the bead tubes with lysate...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the bead tubes with lysate from step 52 for 12000 x g, °C, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Transfer 425 µL supernatant without any...
steps.instruction: Transfer 425 µL supernatant without any particle carryover to the wells of the Deep-well plate. This plate becomes the Sample Plate.

steps.title: Add 540 µL Buffer ACB, and...
steps.instruction: Add 540 µL Buffer ACB, and 25 µL MagAttract Suspension G to each sample in the sample plate. For multiple samples, make a master mix with 10% overage. Invert slowly to mix the master mix, avoid foaming. Add 565 µL mixture to each sample.

steps.title: Select the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash on the instrument
steps.instruction: Select the program IndiMag_Pathogen_KF_Duo_4wash on the instrument.

steps.title: Start the run, then load the...
steps.instruction: Start the run, then load the prepared plates into position when prompted by the instrument.

steps.title: Keep the door open while extraction
steps.instruction: Keep the door open while extraction. The chamber of the KingFisher Duo Prime is small. Closing the door makes the ethanol vapor restrained inside the chamber and increases the ethanol contamination.

steps.title: After the running protocol is completed...
steps.instruction: After the running protocol is completed (~ 00:35:00 ), immediately remove the elution plate from the instrument and cover the plate or transfer the eluate to the final tube or plate of choice for final storage. The elutes from the 2 bead tubes of the same water sample can be pooled in one final tube.

steps.title: In a 0
steps.instruction: In a 0.6 mL microcentrifuge tube, use 3 µL total nucleic acid for DNA and RNA concentration measurement using Qubit 4 Fluorometer following manufacturer instructions (Kits needed: Qubit 1X dsDNA HS Assay Kit and Qubit RNA HS Assay Kit). (see Appendix 5 and Appendix 6).

steps.title: Proceed with sample testing following the...
steps.instruction: Proceed with sample testing following the REDI-NET SOP W-4 Water Testing or store at -20 °C for less than 2 weeks (for long-term storage the sample needs to be stored at -80 °C following the REDI-NET SOP W-3 Water Storage).

steps.title: %s %s PEG-8000 400 g NaCl...
steps.instruction: %s %s PEG-8000 400 g NaCl 70 g Add 1x PBS to final volume 1 L The final concentrations: 40% PEG-8000 and 1.2 Molarity (M) NaCl.

steps.title: Add stir bar, PEG-8000 and NaCl...
steps.instruction: Add stir bar, PEG-8000 and NaCl into an empty 1L sterile bottle (plastic or glass, autoclavable).

steps.title: Add sterile 1xPBS to final volume 1 L
steps.instruction: Add sterile 1xPBS to final volume 1 L .

steps.title: Autoclave the whole bottle with lid...
steps.instruction: Autoclave the whole bottle with lid at 121 °C for 00:30:00 . After autoclave, place the hot solution on a stirring hot plate and stir the solution until it cools down to Room temperature .

steps.title: If autoclave is not available, stir...
steps.instruction: If autoclave is not available, stir the solution on a stirring hot plate until the crystals are fully dissolved and filter the solution through 0.45 µm Corning Disposable Vacuum Filter/Storage Systems.

steps.title: Store the 40% PEG-8000 solution at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Store the 40% PEG-8000 solution at 4 °C .

steps.title: DNA quantification
steps.instruction: DNA quantification : According to the volume of sample used, add the 1xHS dsDNA Qubit Assay for a final volume of 200 µL (i.e., if using 3 µL of sample, add 197 µL of 1x HS dsDNA Qubit Assay. Vortex for 5 -10 seconds, then incubate for 00:02:00 at Room temperature before reading.

steps.title: RNA Quantification :
steps.instruction: RNA Quantification :

steps.title: In a new microcentrifuge tube/falcon tube...
steps.instruction: In a new microcentrifuge tube/falcon tube (depending on the number of samples processed), prepare a working solution of the Qubit HS RNA Assay: %s %s Reagents Volume/sample Volume for n+1 sample Qubit RNA HS Assay buffer 199 µL …. µL Qubit RNA HS Assay Dye 1 µL …. µL

steps.title: In a new 0
steps.instruction: In a new 0.6 ml tube, mix 197 µL of Qubit HS RNA Assay working solution and 3 µL of the sample. Vortex for 00:00:05 , then incubate for 00:02:00 at Room temperature before reading.

steps.title: The reusable magnetic funnel cup (A)...
steps.instruction: The reusable magnetic funnel cup (A) can be replaced by a disposable Microfunnel ST Filter funnel (B) or equivalent. Place the funnel at higher position can accelerate the filtration speed, it can be secured by any available resources as long as the setting is stable. The disposable funnel comes with a 0.45 µm membrane and secures the filter membrane by snap on the filter holding stage. The 0.45 µm can be replaced by a 30 µm , 10 µm , or 5 µm membrane, the support pad under the membrane needs to be kept during the filtration (D). A tube needs to be connected to the inner connector of the Screw Cap GL 45 with 2-hose connector for guide the filtrate to the bottom of the bottle to avoid the water entering the vacuum system (F). A tube clip can be secured to the air outlet tube to control the flowrate if it cannot be controlled through the vacuum pump.

steps.title: APPENDIX 2
steps.instruction: APPENDIX 2. Reference of Water Filtration Speed %s %s The summer collected pond water was filtered through a gauze by gravity to remove all the floating plants. The water samples in this reference had been pre-filtered by 30 µm nylon membrane. A 30 µm nylon membrane can filter a 500 mL very dirty water sample within 00:01:00 - 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Store the 5 µm filter membrane...
steps.instruction: Store the 5 µm filter membrane in a sterile 60 mm Petri dish and keep the dish On ice . Label the Petri dish with sample ID, filtration date, and membrane size.

steps.title: Evenly distribute 250 µL DNA/RNA Shield...
steps.instruction: Evenly distribute 250 µL DNA/RNA Shield Reagent on the membranes in the Petri dishes, seal the Petri dishes with parafilm and store at -20 °C for short-term and -80 °C for long-term until DNA/RNA extraction.

steps.title: Add 187 mL of PEG-8000 solution...
steps.instruction: Add 187 mL of PEG-8000 solution to a 750 mL water sample filtrate (or 62.5 mL PEG-8000 solution to a 250 mL filtrate, the final concentration of PEG-8000 is 8%). Mix well by shaking. If a clean 1 L bottle for filtrate collection is not available, the filtrate can be transferred back to the plastic sampling bag with flat-wire closure for adding PEG-8000.

steps.title: Rinse the magnetic funnel, water inlet...
steps.instruction: Rinse the magnetic funnel, water inlet tube, and 1 L glass bottle with 10% bleach, then wash away the bleach with deionized water.

steps.title: Rinse the inlet tube and 1...
steps.instruction: Rinse the inlet tube and 1 L glass bottle with 70% ethanol, shake off the residuals and allow to air dry. Wipe dry the magnetic funnel with 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Repeat steps from 4 to 19 for another sample
steps.instruction: Repeat steps from 4 to 19 for another sample.

steps.title: To speed up the water filtration,...
steps.instruction: To speed up the water filtration, prepare multiple sets of tubes and clean 1L bottles to avoid the waiting time for the air dry. The magnetic funnel can be used right after the 70% ethanol wipe.

steps.title: After finishing all the sample filtration...
steps.instruction: After finishing all the sample filtration for the day, prepare a positive control for the batch: add 100 µL EBV and 50 µL DENV-1 standard into 180 mL sterile 1x PBS then add 45 mL of PEG-8000 solution.

steps.title: Negative control for the batch of sample filtration
steps.instruction: Negative control for the batch of sample filtration: 180 mL sterile 1x PBS then add 45 mL of PEG-8000 solution.

steps.title: Store PEG-8000-added samples and controls at...
steps.instruction: Store PEG-8000-added samples and controls at 4 °C for more than 04:00:00 or Overnight for the next filtration round (DO NOT store the PEG-8000-added samples at 4 °C more than 24:00:00 that will compromise the sample stability).

steps.title: Evenly distribute 250 µL DNA/RNA Shield...
steps.instruction: Evenly distribute 250 µL DNA/RNA Shield Reagent on the membranes in the Petri dishes, seal the Petri dishes with parafilm and store at -20 °C for short-term and -80 °C for long-term until DNA/RNA extraction.

steps.title: Discard the filtrate
steps.instruction: Discard the filtrate.

steps.title: Rinse the magnetic funnel, water inlet...
steps.instruction: Rinse the magnetic funnel, water inlet tube, and 1 L glass bottle with 10% bleach, then wash away the bleach by running under deionized water.

steps.title: Rinse the inlet tube and 1...
steps.instruction: Rinse the inlet tube and 1 L glass bottle with 70% ethanol, shake off the residuals, and allow to air dry.

steps.title: Wipe dry the magnetic funnel with 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wipe dry the magnetic funnel with 70% ethanol. Repeat steps from 26 to 34 for another sample.

steps.title: Load the sample/bead tubes in the Bullet Blender
steps.instruction: Load the sample/bead tubes in the Bullet Blender.

steps.title: Set the speed at 12 and time at 3
steps.instruction: Set the speed at 12 and time at 3. Press Start.

steps.title: Let the samples settle for 00:01:00...
steps.instruction: Let the samples settle for 00:01:00 and then repeat step 50. Figure 1. The examples of membrane trimming and cutting.

steps.title: After beating, centrifuge the tubes with...
steps.instruction: After beating, centrifuge the tubes with lysate 12000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . STOPPING POINT : lysed samples can be stored at Temperature4 °C DurationOvernight .
